hyperedges.label: Expo SDK 55 Constraint: Expo Go + Prodactivity App + Pin Decision
Android Adaptive Icon Set
hyperedges.rationale: These three images together form the complete Android adaptive icon set for the Prodactivity app: background layer, foreground layer, and monochrome variant.

hyperedges.label: Expo Brand Assets
hyperedges.rationale: These three images are all Expo framework brand/attribution assets bundled with the project: the dark badge, light badge, and logo mark.

hyperedges.label: App Branding Assets - Blue 'A' Logo Identity
Consistent Blue Color Scheme Across Assets
Logo 'A' Mark Variants for Different Contexts
React Logo Resolution Variants
hyperedges.rationale: All three react-logo files are the identical cyan React atom logo at 1x, 2x, and 3x pixel densities for standard and retina displays.

hyperedges.label: All Explore Icon Resolution Variants
hyperedges.rationale: 1x, 2x, and 3x variants of the explore tab icon together form a complete resolution set for all screen densities

hyperedges.label: All Home Icon Resolution Variants
hyperedges.rationale: 1x, 2x, and 3x variants of the home tab icon together form a complete resolution set for all screen densities

hyperedges.label: All Tab Bar Navigation Icons
hyperedges.rationale: Explore and Home icons together compose the tab navigation bar of the Prodactivity app

nodes.label: A short section about me & my life
nodes.norm_label: a short section about me & my life

nodes.label: TLDR; You create habits, add friends and post videos/photos to prove it
nodes.norm_label: tldr; you create habits, add friends and post videos/photos to prove it

nodes.label: TLDR; Instagram is very distracting and you might not like random people seeing your daily life.
nodes.norm_label: tldr; instagram is very distracting and you might not like random people seeing your daily life.

nodes.label: TLDR; Building an ecosystem around these apps. Next: Workout tracker, Notes app and Calendar.
nodes.norm_label: tldr; building an ecosystem around these apps. next: workout tracker, notes app and calendar.

nodes.label: Local-First Architecture
nodes.rationale: App runs fully on-device with no backend by default. Supabase is optional and enables cross-device sync/backup. Without env vars, every remote call is a no-op.

nodes.label: Optimistic Sync Strategy
nodes.rationale: Mutations update local state + AsyncStorage first, then push to backend best-effort. Offline writes retry on next sign-in pull.

nodes.label: Grid Asset (White on White, Likely Icon Placeholder)
nodes.norm_label: grid asset (white on white, likely icon placeholder)

nodes.label: Android Icon Background
nodes.description: Adaptive icon background layer showing a light blue grid/guideline design system template. Displays concentric circles and dashed triangle guidelines on a pale blue background, used as the background layer for Android adaptive icons.

nodes.label: Android Icon Foreground
nodes.description: Adaptive icon foreground layer showing the Prodactivity app logo: a chevron/caret shape (upward-pointing V) in a blue gradient on a transparent background. The shape is the primary brand mark of the Prodactivity app.

nodes.label: Android Icon Monochrome
nodes.description: Monochrome variant of the Prodactivity app icon: a chevron/caret shape (upward-pointing V) in solid grey on a white/transparent background. Used for themed/monochrome Android adaptive icon support.

nodes.label: Expo Badge White
nodes.description: 'Powered by Expo' attribution badge in a light/white variant. Shows text 'Powered by' followed by the Expo logo (chevron mark) and 'Expo' wordmark in black on a white/transparent background.

nodes.label: Expo Badge Dark
nodes.description: 'Powered by Expo' attribution badge in a dark variant. Shows text 'Powered by' followed by the Expo logo (chevron mark) and 'Expo' wordmark in white on a black rounded-rectangle background pill.

nodes.label: Expo Logo
nodes.description: Expo framework logo mark in white on a transparent background. The logo appears as the Expo chevron/caret mark (same shape as the Prodactivity icon foreground), rendered in white, suitable for use on dark backgrounds.

nodes.label: Favicon - Blue 'A' Logo on Light Blue Background
nodes.norm_label: favicon - blue 'a' logo on light blue background

nodes.label: App Icon - 3D White 'A' Logo on Blue Grid Background
nodes.norm_label: app icon - 3d white 'a' logo on blue grid background

nodes.label: Logo Glow Effect - Soft Blue Radial Glow
nodes.norm_label: logo glow effect - soft blue radial glow

nodes.label: Splash Screen Icon - White 'A' Logo on Transparent Background
nodes.norm_label: splash screen icon - white 'a' logo on transparent background

links.rationale: Foreground and background are the two layers of an Android adaptive icon set for the Prodactivity app.
Both the Prodactivity app icon foreground and the Expo logo use a similar upward-pointing chevron/caret shape, suggesting the Prodactivity brand mark is inspired by or derived from the Expo logo.
The monochrome icon uses the same chevron shape as the foreground but in grey, making it a monochrome variant for Android themed icons.
expo-badge-white and expo-badge are light and dark variants of the same 'Powered by Expo' attribution badge.
The Expo logo mark is used as a component inside the white variant of the Expo badge.
The Expo logo mark is used as a component inside both Expo badge images.
Image is the official React atom logo in cyan, directly representing the React brand.
react-logo.png and react-logo@2x.png are the same image at different pixel densities (standard vs 2x retina).
react-logo.png and react-logo@3x.png are the same image at different pixel densities (standard vs 3x retina).
Both assets reside in the same assets/images directory and are part of the Expo/React starter template, likely used together in onboarding or tutorial contexts.
Same React atom logo at 2x retina resolution.
react-logo@2x.png and react-logo@3x.png are resolution variants of the identical React logo.
Same React atom logo at 3x retina resolution.
Screenshot shows the Expo starter web app running at localhost:8081 with a nav bar (Home, Explore, Doc), Expo logo, and 'Welcome to Expo' heading — illustrating the default Expo web UI layout.
Expo is built on React Native/React; the tutorial web screen is part of the React/Expo ecosystem.
@2x is explicitly a 2x retina resolution variant of the base explore icon
@3x is explicitly a 3x retina resolution variant of the base explore icon

links.label: Solid rectangular/card shape icon — likely represents explore or search navigation
links.rationale: The base 1x PNG is the canonical version in the explore icon set
Explore icon is a tab bar icon used in app navigation
@2x is explicitly a 2x retina resolution variant of the base home icon
@3x is explicitly a 3x retina resolution variant of the base home icon

links.label: Solid house/home silhouette icon — represents home navigation
links.rationale: The base 1x PNG is the canonical version in the home icon set
Home icon is a tab bar icon used in app navigation
